content:
2 min read
Just now

--

Some people talk beautifully. Pitch the idea, and heads nod. The market analysis has no holes. Ask about competitors, and the answers come fast — rehearsed, airtight. No question stumps them. Yet years pass, and the business goes nowhere.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Then there’s the opposite. Someone who fumbles the pitch. Stumbles mid-sentence at every presentation. And somehow, the money keeps coming in.
Put the top student on a playground, and something breaks. Not the student — the game. The classroom rewards words. The playground rewards bodies. Different currencies.
We equate good talkers with good thinkers. But talk explains reality. It doesn’t replace it. A business gets one verdict: does the customer open their wallet. No explanation, however beautiful, changes that verdict.
AI makes explanations better. It counters your own arguments. It patches the logic you missed. So it feels like due diligence. Ask, challenge, revise, explain again — a few rounds of this, and it feels like the work is done. But the customer was never in the room.
Language verifies coherence. Reality verifies fit. Sometimes the logic gets so beautiful, you mistake the beauty for the thing itself.
AI doesn’t shrink reality’s value. It raises it. AI can polish an argument forever. Reality rewrites it in a single event. A hundred polished sentences don’t survive one customer’s silence.
We’ll spend more time talking to AI. Our logic will sharpen for it. But if that conversation has no customer, no reader, no other person, no reality in it — we haven’t verified anything. We’ve just gotten better at explaining.
